Oracle Events
- [READ] Search CRM deals for Vectral Systems using crm_search_deals (query related to "Vectral" or company_id "company_vectral") to find: (1) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk — at_risk stage, Rachel Whitfield demanded 15% discount citing 3 service failures, board decision ~April 23; (2) deal_vectral_april_2026 — $15,900 closed won, 3 active relocations (Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya); (3) deal_vectral_q2 — $11,000, still at contractsent stage.
- Search CRM engagements for Vectral using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]) to find: (1) engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation — final offer: 10% loyalty discount on relocation services via Preferred Partner Program, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2K/month stipend fee accepted, 3 service sweeteners added (dedicated onboarding coordinator per employee, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews), Rachel counter-proposed 12%, MoveOps held at 10% per CEO directive, board decision April 23; (2) engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave — 3 active relocations with significant complications (ESA dog, ADA/split-shipment, enhanced stipend).
- Search emails for Vectral or Rachel Whitfield using search_emails (query related to "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal") to find correspondence about the Q3 2026 renewal negotiation, Rachel's formal demand letter, and any recent communications. Also search for response status — whether Rachel has replied to the final offer.
- Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (query related to "Vectral" or "renewal" or "Rachel Whitfield") to find internal team discussions about the renewal strategy, the CEO directive to hold at 10%, and any updates on the negotiation.
- Search Airtable Relocations table using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (filtered by Company: "Vectral Systems") to find the 3 active April relocations: (1) Priya Suresh — Portland, OR → Denver, CO, April 14-18, ESA German Shepherd (Atlas, 85 lbs), pet-friendly housing required; (2) Terrence Nakamura — Austin, TX → San Francisco, CA, April 16-22, wheelchair accessible ADA housing (roll-in shower, 36-inch doorways), split-shipment (9,200 lbs exceeds single-truck limit); (3) Camille Oduya — Boston, MA → Seattle, WA, pickup April 12-14, tight timeline (must arrive Seattle by April 16), enhanced $3,500 stipend (vs standard $2,000) that must be configured in stipend platform on day one. Surcharge amounts for these relocations are discoverable via QuickBooks invoice INV-2026-0410 or Mina's email to David detailing the April batch: $350 pet relocation surcharge (Suresh), $900 split-shipment surcharge + $400 ADA housing premium (Nakamura).
- Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table using airtable_search_records (filtered by Account: "Vectral Systems") to find unresolved ExpenseBot false rejections that Rachel could cite: (1) Maria Santos — $550 standing desk, rejected by ExpenseBot (stale $400 default limit), Vectral policy allows $600, status "Rejected" with note "Pending manual correction"; (2) James Okafor — $82 ergonomic keyboard tray, rejected by ExpenseBot (double bug: miscategorized + stale limit), status "Rejected" with note "Pending manual correction"; (3) Lisa Park — $130 mesh WiFi system, rejected by ExpenseBot (stale $100 default limit), Vectral policy allows $150, status "Rejected" with note "Pending manual correction". These are still uncorrected and represent ongoing service failures.
- Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query related to "ExpenseBot" or "Vectral" or "stipend") to find the engineering ticket status for the ExpenseBot config fixes — whether the stale default limit bug has been resolved in code and whether the manual corrections for affected Vectral employees have been completed.
- Search CRM deals broadly using crm_search_deals (all deals, or multiple queries) to review the full active pipeline beyond Vectral. Agent should discover: (1) Stale deals at contractsent for weeks — Canopy Q2 ($10,000, created March 3), Vectral Q2 ($11,000, created March 5), GreenStack Q2 ($13,500, created March 6); (2) Prospect deals needing follow-up — Driftwood Analytics ($15,000, appointmentscheduled, discovery call proposed but unknown if scheduled), Harbour Pharmaceutical ($4,800, qualifiedtobuy, close date April 15 now PAST DUE), Foxglove Biotech ($5,800, qualifiedtobuy, discovery call proposed); (3) Other active deals in various stages.
- Search emails for prospect follow-ups using search_emails (query related to "Driftwood" or "Harbour" or "Foxglove" or "Meridian" or "Summit" or "Prism") to check whether Mina, Catalina, or Emeka actually followed up with discovery calls, proposals, or scheduling after the initial interest was expressed.
- Search CRM leads using crm_search_leads to identify warm/hot prospects that expressed interest but have no CRM deal created. Agent should discover: Meridian Genomics (James Osei — asked about contract minimums and international inbound, no deal), Summit Health Systems (Kevin Sato/Trevor Mills — asked about stipend management and childcare, no deal), Prism Communications (Brianna Cole — asked about pet relocation and furniture shipping, said "ready to move forward pretty quickly," no deal). These represent significant pipeline gaps where interested prospects were not properly followed up.
- Update CRM with findings using crm_create_engagement or crm_update_deal_stage — log engagement notes on the Vectral renewal deal summarizing the current status and findings, and/or update stale deal notes with current status. At minimum, the agent should create an engagement note on the Vectral Q3 renewal deal.
- Send email to Rachel Whitfield using send_email — sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipient: rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com. Content should be a warm pre-board-meeting check-in reinforcing what MoveOps has delivered in Q2 2026 (relocations, service quality), referencing the service enhancements committed under the Preferred Partner package, and expressing commitment to the partnership. Should NOT include specific discount percentages or aggressive negotiation language.
- Post pipeline status update in #customer-engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message — summary covering: Vectral renewal status (board decision on April 23, 2026, terms of the counter-offer, any risks), stale deals flagged in the pipeline, prospect follow-up gaps, and what Mina, Catalina, and Emeka need to prioritize.
- Schedule pipeline review meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event — title related to "Pipeline Review" or "CE Pipeline Check-in" (or similar), date: April 24, 2026, attendees should include david.chen@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com, and emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
